name: stable_walk
summary: Train a stable, low-oscillation trot that strictly tracks the commanded velocity. Stability comes first; raw speed and aesthetics are secondary.
priorities: Body stays close to upright (small body_orientation_l2 magnitude)
Low body angular velocity and angular momentum
Clean foot contacts with minimal slipping and soft landings
Tracking error is minimized only after the policy is stable
avoid: Aggressive action changes that destabilize the body
Trading away tracking_lin_vel weight to chase shaping rewards
Reward hacking via foot_gait while ignoring orientation
notes: If you must choose between stability and tracking accuracy, choose stability.
